modelDefinitions.name: ConsumptionMode
modelDefinitions.description: List of properties that defines how source material is consumed

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Name of consumption mode

fields.name: maxSerial
fields.description: The maximum serials for a particular component that can be loaded, for use with allowMultiple attribute. REQUIRED, DEFAULT=0 (apply no maximum)

fields.name: adjustmentThreshold
fields.description: Percentage, [0..100] adjustment allowed by autoAdjustment

fields.name: maxUnit
fields.description: Maximum loaded quantity (in productStrategy.conumptionUnit) that can be loaded of an item,REQUIRED, DEFAULT=0 (no minimum)

fields.name: minUnit
fields.description: Minimum loaded quantity (in productStrategy.conumptionUnit) that can be loaded of an item, REQUIRED, DEFAULT=0 (no minimum)

fields.name: allowMixedLots
fields.description: When consuming lotted components , allow for more than one lot to be consumed simultaneously. REQUIRED DEFAULT=true

fields.name: allowMultiple
fields.description: Allow multiple/duplicates of source material

fields.name: autoAdjustment
fields.description: Auto adjust(reconcile/distribute) up to adjustment threshhold

fields.name: intraprocess
fields.description: Material is consumed as neccesary(per production/scrap qunatity) during process

fields.name: noConsumption
fields.description: Component is not consumed

fields.name: phantom
fields.description: Consume subassembly as phantom (consume components)

fields.name: postprocess
fields.description: Material is consumed after process is complete

fields.name: preprocess
fields.description: Material is consumed prior to process

fields.name: requireOrder
fields.description: Require loaded material to be ordered

fields.name: requireSerial
fields.description: require specific resource (inventoryId, assetId)

fields.name: unloadOnComplete
fields.description: Auto unload on completion of process

fields.name: unloadOnDeplete
fields.description: Auto unload on depletion of material

fields.name: validateOnProduction
fields.description: Validate inventory quanties on production

This table is a list of systems flags that are used to describe the different  transaction types.
Links out to cost transaction map

fields.description: Label field

fields.description: Operator activity update

fields.description: System flag that  indicates if a user is logged in

fields.description: System flag that  indicates if the user is logged out

fields.description: System flag that  indicates whether the transaction even is updated

fields.description: System flag that  indicates if Inventory adjustments were done

fields.name: inventoryMove
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if Inventory movement was done

fields.name: materialLoad
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if material has been loaded

fields.name: materialUnload
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if material has been unloaded

fields.name: modeChange
fields.description: System flag that  indicates whether the transaction is in change mode

fields.name: productionAdd
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if production is added

fields.name: productionMaterialLoad
fields.description: Material has been loaded to workcenter (productionSetupMaterial)

fields.name: productionMaterialUnload
fields.description: material has been unloaded from workcenter(productionSetupMaterial)

fields.name: productionSetupAdd
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if a production setup is added

fields.name: productionSetupClear
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if the Production setup is cleared

fields.name: receiptConfirm
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if the receipt is confirmed

fields.name: scrapAdd
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if scrap is added

fields.name: shipmentLoad
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if shipment is loaded

fields.name: shipmentShip
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if shipment is shipped

fields.name: shipmentStage
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if the transaction stage is shipping

fields.name: subEventUpdate
fields.description: System flag that  indicates whether the transaction sub even is updated

fields.name: transferCreate
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if transfer was created

fields.name: transferLoad
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if transfer was loaded

fields.name: transferShip
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if transfer was shipped

fields.name: transferStage
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if transaction is in transfer stage

fields.name: transferUpdate
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if transfer was updated

fields.name: usable
fields.description: System flag that  indicates whether the transaction is usable

fields.name: workOrderComplete
fields.description: System flag that  indicates if work order was completed

fields.name: color
fields.description: Used in visualizations to change the color of transactions matching this type.

modelDefinitions.name: QualityInterval
modelDefinitions.description: Table for specifiying when a quality check should take place. Can be an arbitrary time (shift offset), beginning/end of shift, start/end of process, or during a process specified with a process offset(time), or quantity

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Name - Label field

fields.name: processOffset
fields.description: Time in minutes from start of process(or last qc if repeat) to begin QC check. For use with intraprocess intervals.

fields.name: shiftOffset
fields.description: Time in minutes from start of shift to begin QC check. For use with intrashift intervals.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: The production quantity required to trigger qc check

fields.name: custom
fields.description: Is this a custom type interval. For use with customData JSON field

fields.name: intraProcess
fields.description: QC is during process (while product is actively being made). Typically used in conjunction with product/process quality subject and with process offset field. default=false.

fields.name: intraShift
fields.description: QC is during shift. This would typically used in conjunction with a workcenter subject and the shiftoffset field. default=false.

fields.name: onTransaction
fields.description: Is quality check triggered by transaction? For use with transactionType.

fields.name: postProcess
fields.description: QC  is after  process/process end. Typically used in conjunction with product/process quality subject. default=false.

fields.name: postShift
fields.description: QC at end of shift. This would typically used in conjunction with a workcenter subject. default=false.

fields.name: preProcess
fields.description: QC is before process/ process start (default=false). Typically used in conjunction with product/process quality subject

fields.name: preShift
fields.description: QC at beginning of shift. This would typically used in conjunction with a workcenter subject. default=false.

fields.name: repeat
fields.description: The interval repeats (as per shiftoffset/ processoffset/ quantity). default=false

fields.name: customData
fields.description: Used to define custom parameters associated with qualityInterval.

fields.name: modeId
fields.description: Mode associated with QC

modelDefinitions.name: LocationType
modelDefinitions.description: This is a list of location types

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: This is the name of the location type - Label field

fields.name: binShelf
fields.description: Smaller compartments within rack or shelf locations for storing smaller items, components, or products.

fields.name: bulk
fields.description: Large storage areas for storing large quantities of items in an unstructured manner. Typically used for items that don't require frequent access or picking.

fields.name: crossDocking
fields.description: Areas where items are immediately transferred from receiving to shipping without being placed into long-term storage.

fields.name: drawer
fields.description: mall, drawer-like compartments used for storing small parts, components, or items that require easy access and organization.

fields.name: dynamic
fields.description: Locations equipped with conveyors, carousels, or automated systems for efficient material handling and order fulfillment.

fields.name: floor
fields.description: Storage locations directly on the warehouse floor, suitable for oversized or irregularly shaped items.

fields.name: freezer
fields.description: Locations with sub-zero temperatures for storing frozen goods.

fields.name: hazardousMaterial
fields.description: Specifically designated areas that meet safety and regulatory requirements for storing hazardous or flammable materials.

fields.name: highBay
fields.description: Tall rack-based storage designed for high-density storage of items with minimal picking activity.

fields.name: kitting
fields.description: Locations dedicated to assembling kits or packages of items for specific orders or projects.

fields.name: overflow
fields.description: Temporary storage locations used when primary storage areas are at full capacity.

fields.name: pallet
fields.description: Locations designed to accommodate palletized items. These may include rack-based pallet storage or floor-level pallet storage.

fields.name: picking
fields.description: Dedicated areas designed for order picking. They are often subdivided further into zones to manage order fulfillment efficiently.

fields.name: quarantine
fields.description: Is the location type quality hold?

fields.name: rack
fields.description: High-density storage areas with racks or shelves to efficiently store pallets or cartons.

fields.name: receiving
fields.description: Locations where newly arrived goods are temporarily stored before being put away in their designated storage locations.

fields.name: refrigerated
fields.description: Temperature-controlled storage locations for perishable items or products that require specific temperature conditions.

fields.name: reserve
fields.description: Locations where excess or backup inventory is stored until needed in other parts of the warehouse.

fields.name: shipping
fields.description: Specifically designated for preparing and holding items that are ready to be shipped to customers or other locations.

fields.name: staging
fields.description: Locations used for temporarily holding items before they are shipped or processed further. Staging areas are common near shipping docks.

fields.name: subcontract
fields.description: Location Type can only be used for locations associated with facilities of "outside" these locations are autogenerated by Fuuz and cannot be removed/changed.

fields.name: system
fields.description: System used type - for a flow or other process

fields.name: usable
fields.description: Is the location type usable?

fields.name: workcenter
fields.description: Is the location type a workcenter?

fields.description: working days - default fallback value

fields.name: firm
fields.description: Firm release types - generally are ship schedules which have been confirmed by the customer, to be shippable.

fields.name: forecast
fields.description: Forecast types are generally those that extend for some period beyond the firm ship schedules - usually about 30-60 days - they are generally NOT shippable types - until customer service has converted these (or via edi) to a firm release.

fields.name: planning
fields.description: Planning releases - are generally those that extend far beyond the allowable forecast time frame. For example, if your firms are out 2 weeks and forecasts are out 30 days beyond that, then your planned orders would 'start' around the 45 day mark (from now) and go out as long as necessary for planning purchases/production/etc.

modelDefinitions.description: This table lists the strategy types

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Strategy type name - label field (UNIQUE, REQUIRED, )

fields.name: assembly
fields.description: "Assembly" attribute indicates strategy will be used for manufacturing. Must have at least one BOM component.

fields.name: buy
fields.description: DEPRECATED DO NOT USE - REPLACED WITH INVENTORY

fields.name: byProduct
fields.description:
ByProduct defines the product is not explicity produced - by is the offshoot of another assembly type product.
ByProduct should also include the Inventory boolean

fields.name: child
fields.description:
Child is used when configuring CoProducts - not required for dynamic CoProducts. 

Should also include the Assembly boolean. Limits choices for the user when adding children as outputs to the parent assembly product.

fields.name: inventory
fields.description: Iinventory" attribute indicates strategy is for an inventory item band will have only single process and will not be directly manufactured. Will not have BOM components.

fields.name: make
fields.description: DEPRECATED DO NOT USE - REPLACED WITH ASSEMBLY

fields.name: parent
fields.description: "Parent" attribute indicates strategy that will be used as a container for other products. Inventory with this strategy will not exist except as interprocess WIP..BOM components will be consumed in the final strategy process by "children" -- products configured as Co Products. Parent should also include the "assembly" boolean.

fields.name: phantom
fields.description: It is the product strategy that represent the collection of components.(REQUIRED, DEFAULT = FALSE)

fields.name: usable
fields.description: Usable indicates whether the Strategy type is usable (REQUIRED, DEFAULT=TRUE)

modelDefinitions.name: AssetDesign
modelDefinitions.description: An Asset Design represents an abstract definition of an asset, generally, when an engineering drawing exists for a particular asset - that would be the "design". Generic asset (i.e. non-serialized) can be associated to products, as just an asset design. Asset designs themselves, do not get inventoried, they do not have maintenance performed against them.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: maintenanceIntervalCycles
fields.description: Maintenance interval machine / asset cycles - based on production history for an asset instance associated to this Design - and also must be linked directly to a workcenter if tracking the workcenter as an Asset in Fuuz also, or be associated to a production run via linkage as validated asset to the run, such as a tool or fixture.

fields.name: maintenanceIntervalHours
fields.description: Maintenance interval machine / asset hours- based on workcenter history for an asset instance associated to this Design - and also must be linked directly to a workcenter if tracking the workcenter as an Asset in Fuuz also, or be associated to a production run via linkage as validated asset to the run, such as a tool or fixture.

fields.name: maxLifetimeCycles
fields.description: Maximum number of lifetime cycles for this asset which defines its 'useful life'.

fields.name: maxLifetimeHours
fields.description: Maximum number of production hours for this asset design, which defines its 'useful life'.

fields.name: triggerMaintHrs
fields.description: When True, consider the maintenance hours interval for auto creation of maintenance work orders against individual Assets linked, which have met or exceeded the interval hours since its last maintenace reset.

fields.name: drawingNumber
fields.description: Engineering drawing number for tracking.

fields.name: drawingRevision
fields.description: Engineering drawing number revision for tracking.

fields.name: number
fields.description: This is a system generated sequence number to ensure uniqueness across all asset designs.

fields.name: lastRevisionAt
fields.description: The date of the last revision for this asset design

fields.name: releasedAt
fields.description: The date this asset design was released

fields.name: customer
fields.description: If this is a customer owned asset, identify the customer here.

fields.name: supplier
fields.description: If asset is owned by a supplier, indciate the supplier here.

modelDefinitions.name: KanbanCard
modelDefinitions.description:
KanbanCards   Lean Cards are the "demand" drivers for a board. When Active Cards in a "Available" state exist - production can be applied to an individual card. Once a card enters a "consumed" state it is no longer considered active and a leveling method will be applied to determine when a new card should be added to the board.
Links out to production history

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: number
fields.description: This is the kanban card number

fields.name: available
fields.description: Cards attached to Inventory are not available.

fields.name: oneTime
fields.description: One time cards are used as a single use card, once inventory has been built and consumed, this card will no longer be available for production. Once the associated inventory has been depleted or inactivated, this card will be flipped to inactive.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: When true - this card will be prioritized from a production standpoint.

fields.name: shelfLifeDays
fields.description:
if Shelf Life Control == True & Shelf Life Days = Null or 0, the shelf life for a lot will not auto calculate & is user defined on the lot as they are created.

If Shelf Life Control == False, nothing is done, shelf life calculations are not considered for lots

If Shelf Life Control == True & Shelf Life Days > 0, then shelf life will be calcualted for each lot produced based on 'received or produced date/time' + Shelf Life Days to determine expiration date of Lot.

modelDefinitions.name: WorkOrderProcessGroup
modelDefinitions.description:
Intra Workcenter WO Process Groups. 

Provides a mechanism to consolidate individual work order processes, which need to occur at the same workcenter - to consider things like common tooling/assets/product attrbutes/etc.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: number
fields.description:
A system generated sequenc number to represent this group.

Default = Sequence (string)

fields.name: dispatchSequence
fields.description:
Dispatch Sequence is used to 'order' groups within a dispatch list. When using this field, the dispatch sequence takes precedence over any date field.

Default = 0 (INT)

fields.name: aggQuantity
fields.description:
Aggregate (sum) of all WorkOrderProcess Qty in the group. This field is non-user editable.

Default = 0

fields.name: aggRunDuration
fields.description:
Aggregate Run Duration - is ALWAYS the SUM of all work order process durations within the group - regardless of sequentialOutput.

Default = 0

fields.name: aggSetupDuration
fields.description:
Aggregated (sum) of all setRates within the group. This field is non-user editable.

Default = 0

fields.name: productionDuration
fields.description:
Run duration for this group - this is the duration to be used for scheduling purposes - this run duration should NOT include any setup time, purely production time - based upon the standardRate from the work order processes within the group.

runDuration - also considers if sequentialOutput is true - when true, the run duration is the sum of all the outputs.

Default = 0

fields.name: setupDuration
fields.description:
The actual setup time used for this group - This may not be = the aggSetupRate - if 'single setup rate' is being used, if single setup rate is used, than the longest SetupRate from all of the workOrderProcesses within the Group should be used (max.). This value should ONLY represent the "initial" setup time required for this group - any minor setup time (between work order processes should not be counted here).

This setup time, can be manually adjusted by the user - prior to scheduling the Group - but after scheduling is completed this must be read Only.

Default = 0

fields.name: locked
fields.description:
When locked, the group may not be edited.

Default = False

fields.name: sequentialOutput
fields.description:
Sequential output refers to the sequencing of the work order processes within a given work order process group.

When True - all work order processes will be completed finish to start. Logic: Sum of all work order process durations = Group duration.

When False - the group will be completed in parallel: Logic:
Longest running work order process duration = Group duration.

Default = True

fields.name: singleSetupDuration
fields.description:
When True (managed by user at time of creating the group) - then aggregate (average) all the setupRates of all associated work Order Processes within the group.

use the setupRate field, to indicate the "true" initial setup time for the entire group.

Default = False

fields.name: useMinorSetupDuration
fields.description:
Minor Setup durations - are additional / incremental setup durations required between work order processes -

ONLY applies - when sequentialOutput = True 
AND
when the timeToStart field on the WorkOrderProcess is > 0 (seconds)

When these conditions are met - the Schedule Output - needs to include the total of all the timeToStart durations as "additionalSetupDuration" to provide the accurate 'overall run time' for the work order process group. 

When updating / creating Work Order Process distributions - for each work order process within the Group - the start/end and durations of those individual distributions must incorporate the value from the timeToStart field. This value maybe unique for each work order process within the group.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: ExternalId with a 3rd party system - must be unique!

fields.name: earliestStartAt
fields.description:
The EarliestStartAt is = the Earliest Start At date/time from the collection of Work Order Processes within the Group. This represents the abosolute earliest possible date/time this group can be scheduled to start at.

Field maybe edited by the user, this would impact the scheduling. This field makes no impact after scheduling has been completed.

REQUIRED (no default in API)

fields.name: latestEndAt
fields.description:
LatestEndAt is the latest date from the collection of Work Order Processes within the Group. This represents the absolute due date for this entire group - to be considered by scheduling.

Field maybe edited by the user, this would impact the scheduling. This field makes no impact after scheduling has been completed.

REQUIRED (no default in API)

fields.name: scheduledEndAt
fields.description:
The date/time determined by scheduling of when this group will finish, This date/time should never exceed the latestEndAt - if it does, this should generated a scheduling exception.

This field must be read Only after scheduling is completed - only deleting scheduled events for this group will delete this value.

fields.name: scheduledStartAt
fields.description:
ScheduledStartAt is the date/time determined by scheduling of when this group can start based on workcenter capacity. This date should never be earlier than the earliestStartAt date/time - otherwise, this should generate a scheduling exception.

This field must be read Only after scheduling is completed - only deleting scheduled events for this group will delete this value.

modelDefinitions.name: AssetGroup
modelDefinitions.description:
Asset Groups are "Assemblies" of individual Assets. This may represent a tool, mold, workcell or other collection of assets. Some Asset Groups may represent 1 instance of an assembly (if using the Serial Number). The assets within a group, maybe swapped in / out - by your tooling/maintenance department - whichever assets are part of the group, during production - will get usage applied to them.

If you are ONLY tracking Asset Group/Assemblies - the recomendation is to create an AssetGroup:Asset - since production usage will only be tracked against Assets; alternatively, you can interchange the concept of the AssetGroup for just Assets (skip creating groups).

fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: This is a unique name to identify this Asset Group (Assembly 123-1 for example)

fields.name: barcode
fields.description: This field can be used to perform client side validation of the Asset Group - the barcode string should be 1:1 with an affixed barcode on the asset Group - so when scanned by the system, this can be used to lookup the exact instance without API query.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Notes can be tracked here that you may want to present to operators.

fields.name: number
fields.description: System generated sequence number.

fields.name: serialNumber
fields.description: The globally unique identification of this Asset Group (or asset assembly)

fields.name: inServiceAt
fields.description: The date when this Asset Group was initially placed into service.

fields.description:
Aggregate duration per unit of production representing the ideal rate used for oee calculations.

All distributions within the group are averaged to come up with this figure. seconds per unit.

fields.name: setupAggregate

fields.name: standardAggregate

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: setBatch
fields.description:
Set Batch - allows the user to define the batch size, currently targeted. A single setup may have mutliple batches that get produced. This quantity is used to determine whether the source components added, meet the minimum requirements of the batch size.

For example - in the BOM user defines the Qty of source component per single unit of output in production - if batches are in Lbs - and setBatch = 2500lbs - multiply this by the bom component usage quantities to determine the appropriate amount of each component required to satisfy the batch size.

fields.description: Container ouput size. Copied from work order process distribution "standard pack quantity" or set via the control panel UI

fields.name: virtual

fields.name: earnedMachineDuration

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: number
fields.description: Sequence - shared between multiple production Runs, if co-production

fields.name: availability
fields.description: availability of the workcenter during this production run - based upon the Workcenter History entries from run start to run end.

fields.name: counterAccum
fields.description: This is the final value from the counterAccum when the Run has been ended. This will provide information about the total number of machine cycles captured (via PLC or manually input), during the run.

fields.name: oee
fields.description: oee for this run, calculated at the end of the run.

fields.name: performance
fields.description: Performance calc for this run, calculated at the end of the run

fields.name: quality
fields.description: Quality measurement for this production run, calculated at the end of the run.

fields.name: crewAggregate
fields.description: Crew Aggregate is the sum of all production output - standard operator crew sizes.

fields.name: produced
fields.description:
Total produced where includeInProductionCounts = 1

This  is the total throughput for the entire production run in good units made.

fields.name: runCrewSize
fields.description:
Pull from productionSetup.crewSize when Setup is Cleared.

Note: the crew size at the productionSetup may apply to multiple outputs.

fields.name: scrap
fields.description: Total Scrap where includeInProductionCounts = 1

fields.name: totalProduction
fields.description: This is the total production for the run - including all included production + scrap entries.

fields.name: totalRequired
fields.description: The aggregate of all required quantities across all outputs for this run.

fields.name: endAt
fields.description: The actual date/time of when this Run ended (ie. production setup cleared)

fields.name: startAt
fields.description: The actual date/time of when this Run began (ie production setup added)

fields.name: averageCycleDuration
fields.description: based on actual output (total units) / total production time

fields.name: changeoverAggregate
fields.description: Sum of all Changeover times from all work order process dist groups linked to the run.

fields.name: earnedLaborDuration
fields.description: sum of all earned labor hours - from all work order process dist groups. based on actual output

fields.name: earnedMachineDuration
fields.description: sum of all earned machine hours from all associated work order process dist groups. based on actual output.

fields.name: idealAggregate
fields.description: Ideal time for the run - based on all the workorderprocess dist groups

fields.name: runDuration
fields.description: Total overall duration of the run start to end

fields.name: standardAggregate
fields.description: aggregate of the total number of standard hours expected for the run.

modelDefinitions.name: ProductStrategy
modelDefinitions.description:
This table lists the product strategy attributes
Links out to lean board, work order, inventory, production operator workcenter, product strategy process, co product and, purchase order line

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Label field ( name- version) - (REQUIRED)

fields.name: minBatch
fields.description: min batch will establish the minimum work order qty to be produced.

fields.name: stdBatch
fields.description:
The Standard Batch value - sets the multiplier to use when creating a work order. For example:

stdBatch = 150; work orders should be created in increments of 150.

fields.name: byProduct
fields.description: byProduct - this must have only 1 process - no BOM, the default process = By-Product

fields.name: kanban
fields.description: When Kanban is true, for a strategy, work orders can not be generated via the standard work order creation process, rather you must create Kanban racks for each process within the strategy.

fields.name: leading
fields.description:
Leading - this is a blend of      phantom and manufactured - when Primary - you can only have 1 process,      with or without a BOM - but MUST defined coProducts (outputs) for that      process. The process; need to be the same as the 1st strategyProcess of      the coProducts added.

- If the Leading product       strategy has a bom- it will be consumed by the coProducts; in addition to       anything that is defined in the BOMs of the CoProducts individually as       well.

the default process = Leading

fields.name: manufactured
fields.description: Manufactured - this will have at least 1 process, and must have a BOM on the first process.

fields.name: nonInventory
fields.description: Non Inventory - this will have NO process or BOM, therefore ineligible to have inventory on hand

fields.name: phantom
fields.description:
Phantom - this is similar to manufactured - must have only 1 process - with a BOM - note it is not best practice to have multiple levels of phantoms in a single BOM - if you have multiple stages of production that are not being tracked you should just flatten all the BOM consumption into a single phantom product. (ie do not have a Phantom, consuming another phantom)

default process = Phantom

fields.name: purchased
fields.description: Purchased - this will have only 1 process - with NO bom, the default process = Purchase

fields.name: schedule
fields.description: If True - this strategy will be inelligible for kanban production. If False, you will not be allowed to schedule Work Orders for this Strategy.

fields.name: validated
fields.description: Has this strategy been validated for use? If false (default) strategy cannot be used for production or for the generation of work order processes. Once set updates to Product Strategy will force a new version. (REQUIRED)

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: ManufacturingRoutingId associated with the assembly if using advanced Routings

fields.name: name
fields.description: Product Strategy name (REQUIRED)

fields.name: revision
fields.description: Product strategy revision (REQUIRED)

fields.name: productId
fields.description: Identifies the product (REQUIRED)

fields.name: product
fields.description: Product object (REQUIRED)

fields.description: Identifies the consumption unit

fields.description: Inventory unit object

fields.name: purchaseUnit
fields.description: Purchse unit object

fields.name: saleUnitId
fields.description: Identifies the sale unit

fields.name: saleUnit
fields.description: Sale unit object

fields.name: unitTypeId
fields.description: Identifies the unit type

fields.name: unitType
fields.description: Unit type object

fields.description: Link to work orders

modelDefinitions.name: Area
modelDefinitions.description:
"Area" is defined as a physical, geographical, or logical grouping within a site. This grouping is determined by the site and can include various work centers such as process cells, production units, production lines, and storage zones.
A larger section within the facility, often dedicated to a particular function or process.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The unique identifier for the Area.

fields.name: process
fields.description: These are areas where specific manufacturing processes take place, such as mixing, heating, or assembly.

fields.name: productionCell
fields.description: These are smaller, more specific areas within a process area where particular tasks or operations are performed. An Example maybe "CNC Department" within this Area you may have different Groups of CNCs such as Lathe, Mill, etc. which can be further broken down by child areas and then workcenter Groups as necessary.

fields.name: productionLine
fields.description: These are linear arrangements of equipment and workstations where products move through various stages of production.

fields.name: storage
fields.description: These areas are designated for storing raw materials, work-in-progress items, or finished goods.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A brief explanation of the Area's purpose or function.

fields.name: parentAreaId
fields.description: The ID of the parent Area to which the sub-area belongs.

fields.name: subAreas
fields.description: Areas that are defined with a parent area.

fields.description: The sequence of the work order step - helps to drive sequencial manufacturing processes. Allows for reordering of steps - provided that the work order has not yet been started.

fields.description: use to define a standard production/standard package qty for this product. modelDefinitions.description:
This table is a collection of attributes needed for the Product Strategy Process
Unique indeces: productID, strategyID and, order
Links out to: product strategy process BOM, inventory, productopn operator workcenter, co product, approved workcenter, approved supplier and, work order process

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: distribution
fields.description: distribution % for the primary workcenter defined here on the PSP. This is an integer value. 50 = 50%; default will be set to 100%

fields.name: sequence
fields.description: Sequence integer value for the process

fields.name: productionOperators
fields.description: The number of operators required to use the work center for production.

fields.name: setupOperators
fields.description: The number of operators required to setup the work center for this product.

fields.name: standardPackQuantity
fields.description: Standard Packaging Quantity for this Process. UOM based on the inventory UOM for this product Strategy.

fields.name: unitWeight
fields.description: Weight per unit (DEFAULT = 0)

fields.name: active
fields.description: System flag indicates whether the product strategy process is active (REQUIRED, DEFAULT = TRUE)

fields.name: description
fields.description: description

fields.name: extProcessCode
fields.description: This is only used when an external process does not have an exact match to a Fuuz process ID. This field is not required and not unique.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: Links out to other system

fields.name: customData
fields.description: A place to store multiple attributes or pieces of custom data for the ProductStrategyProcess.

fields.name: cycleTime
fields.description: Seconds of machine time to produce 1 unit of product.

fields.name: idealDuration
fields.description: Ideal duration is the amount of time to produce 1 unit of output, at ideal OEE rate.

fields.name: processDuration
fields.description: Process duration - the fixed amount of time required to perform this process, also used for OSP lead time.

fields.name: setupDuration
fields.description: Time required to perform a setup for this process, regardless of the production batch size.

fields.name: standardDuration
fields.description: Standard duration is the amount of time required to produce 1 unit of output.

fields.name: transitAfter
fields.description: Transit time required after performing this process.

fields.name: transitBefore
fields.description: Transit time required before beginning this process.

fields.name: processId
fields.description: Identified the process (REQUIRED)

fields.name: process
fields.description: Process object (REQUIRED), the 'Default' process maybe used here, if the incoming data does not have a processID match in Fuuz.

fields.name: productStrategyId
fields.description: Identifies the product strategy (REQUIRED)

fields.name: productStrategy
fields.description: ProductStrategy object (REQUIRED)

fields.name: documentDesignId
fields.description: Identifies the document

fields.name: documentDesign
fields.description: document object this document is the primary label to be generated for inventory when created in Fuuz.

fields.name: putAwayLocationId
fields.description: Default location to put away inventory produced by thie productStrategyProcess

fields.name: putAwayLocation
fields.description: PutAwayLocation object

fields.name: workcenterId
fields.description: Workcenter that process will be run on

fields.name: workcenter
fields.description: workCenter object

fields.name: inventory
fields.description: Link to inventory

fields.name: dataChanges
fields.description: A relation to the data changes for this document.

modelDefinitions.name: Asset
modelDefinitions.description:
Assets - each asset is an instance of an asset design, if the assets are serialized.

If assets are not serialized, you need only create 1 asset to represent the asset. Only Assets in Fuuz will track production usage (out of the box). You can use Asset Groups, to define an "assembly" of assets - like a mold/die - with assets being individual details or components that you want to track. If you only want to track the overall assembly (for usage) - just set it up as an asset (skip asset group).

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: number
fields.description: System generated sequence number.

fields.name: currentCycles
fields.description: number of cycles since the last maintenance was performed

fields.name: lifetimeCycles
fields.description: Number of cycles on this asset.

fields.name: maintenanceIntervalCycles
fields.description: this is the number of impressions that can be applied to this asset during production (includes production + scrap outputs) to trigger a preventive maintenance request. If set to 0 no request will be generated.

fields.name: maxLifetimeCycles
fields.description: Maximum number of lifetime cycles for this asset (useful life).

fields.name: topLevel
fields.description: Top level assets are able to be joined to other objects in Fuuz - such as Workcenters, Product Strategies and anything that may link to an Asset.

fields.name: barcode
fields.description: This field can be used to perform client side validation of the Asset - the barcode string should be 1:1 with an affixed barcode on the asset - so when scanned by the system, this can be used to lookup the exact instance without API query.

fields.name: serialNumber
fields.description: Each asset requires a serial number. If not provided by the manufacturer, the system will assign a sequential number. This will be used for all maintenance activities.

fields.name: inServiceAt
fields.description: Date when the asset was placed into service at your facility originally.

fields.name: newAt
fields.description: Date when the asset was new.

fields.name: nextMaintenanceDueAt
fields.description: The date when the next scheduled maintenace is due to be performed.

fields.name: purchasedAt
fields.description: The date when this asset was purchased by your company.

fields.name: retiredAt
fields.description: The date when this asset was retired or taken out of service.

fields.name: accumHours
fields.description: Accumulated hours for this asset are all the production hours over the known life of the asset. This value may only ever be reset manually - for example, if a complete refurbish were done and you want to reset the clock.

fields.name: maintenanceHours
fields.description: Number of production hours to trigger a maintenance request for this asset. This is an aggregation of all production run hours, minus any disabled hours. This field, replaced the previous field "maintenanceIntervalHours".

fields.name: maxHours
fields.description: Maximum number of lifetime hours for this asset (usable life). Replaced previous field "maxLifetimeHours".

fields.name: totalHours
fields.description: Total accumulated hours of this asset since the last time this value was reset. Total hours are an accumulation of all production hours for the asset, when the asset is associated to production runs. idle/downtime are not included in this amount.

modelDefinitions.name: WorkOrder
modelDefinitions.description:
This table lists work orders and it's attributes
Links out to transfer order line, work order process, work order process  distribution and, work order process BOM

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: number
fields.description: Label field, and the number of the Work Order. This should also be the ID of the workorder.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: The priority of Work Order

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: The desired output quantity for the overall Work Order.

fields.name: active
fields.description: Generally triggered by the state of the linked Kanban Rack.

fields.name: buildToOrder
fields.description: Build to order implies that this work order product, is a finished good and is necessary to meet a specific customer shipment.

fields.name: buildToStock
fields.description: Build to stock implies that there are not pending customer requirements, however, this product will be used to satisfy minimum inventory levels in the warehouse.

fields.name: kanban
fields.description: Kanban orders are repetative - this type of order should not be manually created - rather - use the Kanban module to maintain this type of order.

fields.name: maintenance
fields.description: Maintenance orders are to perform preventive or unplanned maintenance on an asset. These should not be manually created through the UI, but should be initiated from the Maintenance module.

fields.name: customerReference
fields.description: Use this field to identify the Customer associated with this work order - when you are not using Fuuz customer master data. This field can be populated manually - or through integration. Nothing in this field will be validated - it is free form.

fields.name: extProduct
fields.description: String value of an Extenally defined product or item for manufacturing - this is only used, if products are not being used within Fuuz.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: A link to a record from an external system.

fields.name: newField
fields.description: this is my new field

fields.name: orderReference
fields.description: Use this field to identify the Order associated with this work order - when you are not using Fuuz sales order transaction data. This field can be populated manually - or through integration. Nothing in this field will be validated - it is free form.

fields.name: actualFinishAt
fields.description: Actual Date/Time of the final Work Order Processes, final distribution, or the date/time of when/if a Work Order was prematurely closed via status change of the Work Order.

fields.name: actualStartAt
fields.description: Actual Date/Time of the intiail 'setup' of the first process distribution of the Work Order.

fields.name: scheduledFinishAt
fields.description: The date this work order is scheduled to finish production - System Generated

fields.name: scheduledStartAt
fields.description: The date this work order is scheduled to start production - System Generated

fields.name: productId
fields.description: Identifies the product

fields.name: product
fields.description: Product object

fields.name: productStrategyId
fields.description: Identifies the product strategy

fields.name: productStrategy
fields.description: Product strategy object

fields.description: Linkd to work order process

modelDefinitions.name: ApplicationSpanEventLog
modelDefinitions.description: The Application Span Event Log Event model will collect information about what ran a mutation. The specific Application Span Event Log Id can be found on any Application models through the _trace field. This will only capture trace information for span events that run a mutation.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: spanId
fields.description: The ID of the parent span, this is not a relation. When you group by the spanId you can see all the child requests that were created under the parent.

fields.name: originServiceName
fields.description: The service the request originates from.

fields.name: forwardedForServiceName
fields.description: The service the request was forwarded through. Through a trace, this could create a chain of services the request was forwarded through.

fields.name: eventType
fields.description: The type of event that was logged. Ex: 'Mutation'

fields.name: traceId

fields.description: The name of the Data Flow.

fields.description: The description of the data flow.

fields.description: Whether the data flow is active and should run once deployed.

fields.name: substitutesDataFlowId
fields.description: The unique identifier of the Data Flow this Data Flow substitutes. If the current Data Flow is active and deployed, it will replace the target Data Flows effectiveDeploymentId with it's currentDeploymentId

fields.name: substitutesDataFlow
fields.description: The relation to the Data Flow this Data Flow substitutes. If the current Data Flow is active and deployed, it will replace the target Data Flows effectiveDeployment with it's currentDeployment

fields.description: The relation to the Data Flow that substitutes this Data Flow. If the Data Flow is active it will replace the current Data Flows effectiveDeploymentId with it's currentDeploymentId

fields.name: dataFlowTypeId
fields.description: The unique identifier of the type of the data flow.

fields.name: dataFlowType
fields.description: The type of the data flow.

fields.name: logLevelId
fields.description: The identifier of the log level specified that defines which logs are captured. Example: "Info" - only Info, Warn, Error and Fatal will be captured ignoring Debug and Trace logs.

fields.name: logLevel
fields.description: The log level specified will define which logs are captured. Example: "Info" - only Info, Warn, Error and Fatal will be captured ignoring Debug and Trace logs.

fields.description: The configuration data necessary to expose this data flow to LLM agents via the MCP server as a tool.

fields.name: versions
fields.description: The versions of this data flow.

fields.name: deployments
fields.description: The deployments of this data flow.

fields.name: schedules
fields.description: The Data Flow Schedules for this Data Flow

fields.name: currentDeploymentId
fields.description: The unique identifier of the current deployment of this data flow.

fields.name: currentDeployment

modelDefinitions.name: DataFlowMCPToolConfiguration
modelDefinitions.description: The DataFlowMCPToolConfiguration type includes the configuration information necessary to expose a data flow as a tool in the MCP server.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether this data flow should be enabled for LLM agents to execute via the MCP server.

fields.name: inputSchema
fields.description: A JSON schema defining the shape of the input the LLM agent needs to supply to the tool. This input becomes the payload to the data flow. This schema is used by the LLM agent to format the input, and to determine if this tool is the correct one to call.

fields.name: outputSchema
fields.description: A JSON schema defining the shape of the output the tool will return to the LLM. The data flow output is validated against this schema prior to returning the output to the agent. This schema is used by the LLM agent to determine if this is the correct tool to call.

fields.name: readOnly
fields.description: If true, the tool does not modify its environment.

fields.name: destructive
fields.description: If true, the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment. If false, the tool performs only additive updates. This property is meaningful only when readOnly is set to false.

fields.name: idempotent
fields.description: If true, calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments will have no additional effect on the its environment. This property is meaningful only when readOnly is set to false.

fields.name: _aggregate
fields.description: Aggregated data for the node, grouped by the selected node fields.

fields.description: The name for the schedule, the name should represent the schedules purpose.

fields.name: active
fields.description: The active field indicates whether the schedule should run. If the value is true, the schedule will be added, if false the schedule will be ignored.

fields.name: inputSchema
fields.description: A JSON Schema which defines the inputs that can be used for a schedule and render inputs on the frequencies form dialogs. Additionally the inputSchema is used to validate the payload provided on the frequency before the schedule executes. If an invalid payload is provided on the frequency. The frequency will be updated with a status that indicates the issue.

modelDefinitions.name: DataModelCustomField
modelDefinitions.description: The DataModelCustomField model represents a custom field on an MFGx data model.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the Data Model Custom Field.

fields.name: label
fields.description: The label of the custom field. This label will be displayed to users in the UI as input and table column labels.

fields.name: apiFieldName
fields.description: The name of the custom field. This name will be used in the GraphQL API. It should be unique across all custom fields in the same Data Model and cannot be changed after the field is created.

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the Data Model Custom Field. This description will be displayed to users in the UI in field-level help.

fields.name: deprecationReason
fields.description: A explanation of why this field is deprecated. Fields with a deprecation reason set are considered deprecated and should not be used in queries or mutations, but are not removed from the API.

fields.name: showOnTables
fields.description: Whether to show this custom field on forms which use the Custom Fields Column screen element.

fields.name: showOnForms
fields.description: Whether to show this custom field on forms which use the Custom Fields Input screen element.

fields.name: groupLabel
fields.description: The label of the group (if any) to place this custom field in on tables and forms. Fields with the same group label are placed under a shared heading. Groups provide a simple mechanism for organizing custom fields.

fields.name: dataModelName
fields.description: The name of the Data Model this custom field should appear on.

fields.name: dataModel
fields.description: The Data Model this custom field should appear on. This relation will only resolve if the data model is a custom model.

fields.name: dataModelCustomFieldTypeId
fields.description: The ID of the type of this data model custom field.

fields.name: dataModelCustomFieldType
fields.description: The type of this data model custom field.

fields.name: graphQLType
fields.description: The GraphQL type string used for this field in the GraphQL API. This is set automatically on field creation based on the selected custom field type.

modelDefinitions.name: SchedulingConfiguration
modelDefinitions.description: Scheduling Configuration is defined for the SchedulingConfiguration Screen Element as well as the Schedule Engine. The definition defined on a record of this model will provide loading data into the scheduler as well as committing that data back to the database. This configuration will be copied upon creation of a SchedulingConfigurationRun.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of your Scheduling Configuration. This will generally align with the use case of the Scheduling Configuration.

fields.name: schedulingWindow
fields.description: The Scheduling Window will define what part of the schedule can be changed from within the context of a scheduling run.

fields.name: dependencyMapping
fields.description: Dependencies define relations between events (Ex: A -> B when A is completed, B can be started). Dependency Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load dependencies into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: eventMapping
fields.description: Events are the range of time defined on the schedule (Ex: Work Order Process). Event Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load events into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: resourceMapping
fields.description: Resources are the units that you allocate the events to (Ex: Workcenter). Resource Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load resources into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: assignmentMapping
fields.description: Assignments are the relation between Events and Resources, defining what is currently assigned to a resource. Assignment Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load assignments into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: uiOptions
fields.description: Defines the User Interface options for the scheduler

fields.name: options
fields.description: Defines options use for the Scheduler, these will be shared across the front and backend.

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationRuns
fields.description: The relation to the SchedulingConfigurationRuns created based on the configuration of this record.

modelDefinitions.name: SchedulingConfigurationRun
modelDefinitions.description: The Scheduling Configuration Run represents a scheduling attempt on the resources defined by the mappings provided in the Scheduling Configuration. When a Scheduling Configuration Run is created, it will automatically begin the process of scheduling the provided resources by automatically loading them into the Scheduler, processing them and updating the run record with the changes the scheduler made. From there you can commit those changes through the `commitSchedulingConfigurationRun` mutation.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: label
fields.description: The label can be used to assist in searching for a specific run. If no value is provided it will be defaulted.

fields.name: startDate
fields.description: The calculated start date of the Scheduling Run based on the Configurations Scheduling window. Will always be populated by the start of the day when the run is created.

fields.name: endDate
fields.description: The calculated end date of the Scheduling Run based on the Configurations Scheduling window. Will always be populated with the startDate + schedulingWindow duration

fields.name: schedulingWindow
fields.description: The Scheduling Window will define what part of the schedule can be changed from within the context of a scheduling run. This is copied from the SchedulingConfiguration upon creation of a run.

fields.name: changes
fields.description: The changes reported by Bryntum

fields.name: filter
fields.description: An array of objects containing a dataModel and predicate property used for targeting events to be scheduled.

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationId
fields.description: The ID of the Scheduling Configuration of which this run will be created against.

fields.name: schedulingConfiguration
fields.description: The relation to the Scheduling Configuration

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationRunStatusId
fields.description: The ID of the Scheduling Configuration Run Status the Run is currently in.

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationRunStatus
fields.description: The relation to the Scheduling Configuration Run Status.

fields.name: error
fields.description: If an error occurs during scheduling or committing that error will be reported here.

fields.name: autoCommit
fields.description: When autoCommit is true, the run record will automatically update the Events model with changes provided by the scheduler. This is useful when there is no desire to review the schedule before it's committed.

fields.name: autoSchedule
fields.description: When autoSchedule is true, the Scheduling Engine will automatically solve for the provided constraints and provide a new schedule.

fields.name: dependencyMapping
fields.description: Dependencies define relations between events (Ex: A -> B when A is completed, B can be started). Dependency Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load dependencies into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: resourceMapping
fields.description: Resources are the units that you allocate the events to (Ex: Workcenter). Resource Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load resources into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.
